title: Finished Past vs. Still Connected
description: Learning the difference between things that are OVER and things that are STILL CONNECTED to now.
lesson_content_markdown:
# The Big Divide

Many students find this part tricky. Let's make it simple! There are two ways to talk about the past: 

### 1. The 'Closed Box' (Simple Past)
Use this when the time is **finished** and you say **when** it happened. 
- *"I **finished** my homework **at 9 PM**."*
- *"I **went** to Tokyo **last year**."*
- *"Once, the university **built** a library."*

### 2. The 'Open Bridge' (Present Perfect)
Use this when the time is **not finished** or the action is **still true**.
- *"I **have finished** my homework."* (It is done right now!)
- *"I **have been** to Tokyo."* (At some point in my life, I don't say when.)
- *"The university **has built** a library."* (The library is still there today!)

### The 'When' Test
If your sentence has a 'Time Word' like **yesterday**, **last week**, **at 5:00**, or **in 2023**, you must use the **Closed Box** (Simple Past). If there is no specific time, use the **Open Bridge** (Present Perfect).

mastery_check.prompt_to_student: Which word is correct for each situation?

questions.question: The computer laboratory _____ into the university campus during the 1990s.
Over the past few months, the student government _____ several new budget changes.
The professor _____ for many different international organizations before coming to our university.
